machinery_genes.quote: A biochemical analysis was performed on three components: EscN, a predicted cytoplasmic ATPase; EscV, a predicted inner membrane protein; and EscC, a predicted outer membrane secretin.
machinery_genes.note: Gauthier, Puente & Finlay 2003 (Infect Immun), EPEC E2348/69-derived strain. EscC is the SctC/YscC/InvG/HrcC outer-membrane secretin of the LEE injectisome.

machinery_genes.quote: The basal body is formed by a set of ring structures associated with the inner (EscJ, EscD) and outer (EscC) bacterial membranes and a periplasmic inner rod (EscI).
machinery_genes.note: Romo-Castillo et al 2014 (J Bacteriol), EPEC injectisome. EscJ = SctJ/YscJ inner-membrane lipoprotein ring component.

machinery_genes.quote: The basal body is formed by a set of ring structures associated with the inner (EscJ, EscD) and outer (EscC) bacterial membranes and a periplasmic inner rod (EscI).
machinery_genes.note: Romo-Castillo et al 2014 (J Bacteriol). EscD = SctD/YscD/PrgH inner-membrane ring component.

machinery_genes.quote: The basal body is formed by a set of ring structures associated with the inner (EscJ, EscD) and outer (EscC) bacterial membranes and a periplasmic inner rod (EscI).
machinery_genes.note: Romo-Castillo et al 2014 (J Bacteriol). EscI (rOrf8) = SctI/YscI/PrgJ inner rod; also independently named as the inner rod component in Monjaras Feria et al 2012 (PMID 22923600).

machinery_genes.quote: the basal structure houses the type III export apparatus, composed of five integral inner membrane proteins essential for protein secretion (EscR, EscS, EscT, EscU, and EscV)
machinery_genes.note: Romo-Castillo et al 2014. EscR = SctR/YscR/SpaP (FliP homolog), core export gate.

machinery_genes.quote: the basal structure houses the type III export apparatus, composed of five integral inner membrane proteins essential for protein secretion (EscR, EscS, EscT, EscU, and EscV)
machinery_genes.note: Romo-Castillo et al 2014. EscS = SctS/YscS/SpaQ (FliQ homolog), core export gate.

machinery_genes.quote: the basal structure houses the type III export apparatus, composed of five integral inner membrane proteins essential for protein secretion (EscR, EscS, EscT, EscU, and EscV)
machinery_genes.note: Romo-Castillo et al 2014. EscT = SctT/YscT/SpaR (FliR homolog), core export gate.

machinery_genes.quote: the basal structure houses the type III export apparatus, composed of five integral inner membrane proteins essential for protein secretion (EscR, EscS, EscT, EscU, and EscV)
machinery_genes.note: Romo-Castillo et al 2014. EscU = SctU/YscU/SpaS (FlhB homolog), autocleaving export-gate switch.

machinery_genes.quote: A biochemical analysis was performed on three components: EscN, a predicted cytoplasmic ATPase; EscV, a predicted inner membrane protein; and EscC, a predicted outer membrane secretin.
machinery_genes.note: Gauthier, Puente & Finlay 2003. EscV = SctV/YscV/InvA (FlhA homolog), major export-apparatus ring; also named in Romo-Castillo et al 2014 export-apparatus list.

machinery_genes.quote: A biochemical analysis was performed on three components: EscN, a predicted cytoplasmic ATPase; EscV, a predicted inner membrane protein; and EscC, a predicted outer membrane secretin.
machinery_genes.note: Gauthier, Puente & Finlay 2003. EscN = SctN/YscN/InvC ATPase; ATPase function in EPEC also shown by Gauthier & Finlay 2003 (PMID 14617638) and Romo-Castillo et al 2014.

machinery_genes.quote: EscN forms a complex with EscL (FliH), a putative ATPase-negative regulator, and with EscQ (FliN), a predicted component of the cytoplasmic C ring.
machinery_genes.note: Romo-Castillo et al 2014. EscL = SctL/YscL/OrgB, the FliH-like stator linking the ATPase to the C-ring.

machinery_genes.quote: EscN forms a complex with EscL (FliH), a putative ATPase-negative regulator, and with EscQ (FliN), a predicted component of the cytoplasmic C ring.
machinery_genes.note: Romo-Castillo et al 2014. EscQ = SctQ/YscQ/SpaO cytoplasmic C-ring/sorting platform (LEE gene sepQ/escQ).

machinery_genes.quote: We show that EscO shares similar properties with the flagellar FliJ and the Yersinia YscO protein families... it was found to interact with the ATPase EscN and its negative regulator, EscL, of the export apparatus.
machinery_genes.note: Romo-Castillo et al 2014 (the founding EscO characterization paper). EscO (previously Orf15/EscA) = SctO/YscO, FliJ-like central-stalk component that positively regulates EscN ATPase activity.

machinery_genes.quote: EscC was correctly localized in the escF needle component mutant, indicating that secretin localization is independent of needle formation.
machinery_genes.note: Gauthier, Puente & Finlay 2003 names escF as the needle component; EscF = SctF/YscF/PrgI. Also named as the needle protein in Romo-Castillo et al 2014 and Monjaras Feria et al 2012.

machinery_genes.quote: our results suggest that Orf16 acts as a molecular measuring device in a way similar to that of members of the Yersinia YscP and flagellar FliK protein family. Therefore, we propose that this protein be renamed EscP.
machinery_genes.note: Monjaras Feria et al 2012 (the founding EscP/Orf16 characterization paper). EscP = SctP/YscP/InvJ molecular ruler controlling needle length, partnering with EscU in the early-to-late substrate switch.

machinery_genes.role: needle-extending helical filament (sheath conduit to host)
machinery_genes.quote: In addition, a long helical homopolymeric filament of the EspA protein extends the needle.
machinery_genes.note: Monjaras Feria et al 2012. EspA forms the EPEC/EHEC-specific filamentous extension of the needle that bridges to the host-cell membrane translocon; apparatus (not an effector) per the T3SS brief.

machinery_genes.quote: Upon contact with the host cell, the injectisome assembles a translocation pore formed by the proteins EspB and EspD in the enterocyte cell membrane.
machinery_genes.note: Monjaras Feria et al 2012. EspB = translocator forming the host-membrane pore (counterpart of YopB/SipB/IpaB); apparatus per the T3SS brief, not an effector. (EspB also has a reported secondary intracellular activity, but it is named here as a translocon-pore component.)

machinery_genes.quote: Upon contact with the host cell, the injectisome assembles a translocation pore formed by the proteins EspB and EspD in the enterocyte cell membrane.
machinery_genes.note: Monjaras Feria et al 2012. EspD = translocator forming the host-membrane pore (counterpart of YopD/SipC/IpaC); apparatus per the T3SS brief, not an effector.

machinery_genes.quote: SepL is a known member of the gatekeeper family of proteins, which includes YopN in Yersinia, InvE and SsaL in Salmonella, and MxiC in Shigella.
machinery_genes.note: Shaulov et al 2017 (mBio). SepL = SctW/YopN-family gatekeeper that controls the switch from translocator to effector secretion; apparatus-associated regulator of the secretion gate (distinct from a transcriptional regulator).

notes: System = the LEE (locus of enterocyte effacement) injectisome of EPEC O127:H6 E2348/69, the archetype of the Esc/Sep (SctC-V) T3SS nomenclature. The complete LEE sequence for this exact strain is Elliott et al 1998 (Mol Microbiol, PMID 9593291), but that paper is not open access, so the verbatim component-naming quotes here are drawn from open-access EPEC functional/structural papers that work on the E2348/69 LEE injectisome directly (Gauthier 2003 x2; Monjaras Feria 2012; Romo-Castillo 2014; Shaulov 2017) — no strain transfer needed; all describe the EPEC LEE system itself. Core apparatus fully covered: basal-body rings EscC (OM secretin), EscD + EscJ (IM rings); inner rod EscI; five-protein export apparatus EscR/EscS/EscT/EscU/EscV; cytoplasmic ATPase complex EscN + EscL (stator) + EscQ (C-ring) + EscO (FliJ-like stalk); needle EscF; ruler EscP; needle-extending filament EspA and host-membrane translocon pore EspB/EspD; gatekeeper SepL. Per the T3SS brief, the translocon proteins (EspA/EspB/EspD) are counted as apparatus (they form the needle extension and host-membrane pore), not effectors, even though they are secreted. Deliberately EXCLUDED as non-machinery: the injected effectors Tir, Map, EspF/G/H/Z and the non-LEE Nle effectors (these match the curated effector list for this instance); the secretion chaperones CesT, CesAB(CesA/B), CesD/CesD2, CesF (escort/chaperone, rule 5); and the transcriptional regulators Ler, GrlA, GrlR. EspB is named purely as a translocon-pore component here; its reported secondary cytoplasmic activity is not the basis for inclusion. SepL is included as the apparatus-associated gatekeeper (SctW), not as a transcriptional regulator. No low-confidence padding: every listed gene has a verbatim naming quote tying it to the EPEC LEE T3SS apparatus. All cited PMIDs/DOIs verified via get_article_metadata.
